beginner.question: What are CSS cascade layers?
beginner.answer: CSS cascade layers (@layer) allow you to control the order of precedence for groups of styles, overriding traditional specificity.

beginner.question: How do you define a cascade layer?
beginner.answer: Use the @layer rule: @layer base { body { margin: 0; } } to group styles into a named layer.

beginner.question: What is the purpose of cascade layers?
beginner.answer: Layers let authors control which style declarations win in the cascade regardless of specificity, solving specificity conflicts at scale.

beginner.question: How do you specify the order of cascade layers?
beginner.answer: Declare layer names in order: @layer base, components, utilities; styles in later layers override earlier ones.

beginner.question: What happens when styles are in the same layer?
beginner.answer: Within the same layer, normal cascade rules apply: specificity and source order determine which styles win.

beginner.question: Can you add styles to a layer after it is defined?
beginner.answer: Yes, you can reassign layers later: @layer base { h1 { font-size: 2rem; } } adds new rules to the existing base layer.

beginner.question: What is the cascade order with layers?
beginner.answer: The order is: normal styles in layers (ordered by layer declaration) then normal styles without layers, with each layer evaluated by declaration order.

beginner.question: How do layers interact with !important?
beginner.answer: important reverses layer precedence: later layers lose to earlier layers when !important is used, making earlier layers win.

beginner.question: Can you nest layers inside other layers?
beginner.answer: Yes, use dot notation: @layer ui.components { ... } creates a sublayer ui.components inside the ui layer.

beginner.question: How do unlayered styles compare to layered styles?
beginner.answer: Unlayered styles have higher priority than layered styles in the normal cascade (without !important).

intermediate.question: What is a common layering strategy for large projects?
intermediate.answer: A typical strategy: @layer reset, base, components, utilities, overrides; each layer builds on the previous with increasing specificity priority.

intermediate.question: How do you use @layer with @import?
intermediate.answer: Use @import url('file.css') layer(name); to assign imported stylesheets directly to a specific cascade layer.

intermediate.question: How do layers affect third-party stylesheet integration?
intermediate.answer: Import third-party CSS into an early layer (e.g., @layer vendor) so your styles automatically override them without specificity hacks.

intermediate.question: How does the cascade layer order interact with media queries?
intermediate.answer: Layer order is established globally, not per media query; styles from a later layer win over earlier layers across all media contexts.

intermediate.question: Can you change layer order after layers have been used?
intermediate.answer: No, the first @layer statement that declares the order is final; subsequent @layer references cannot reorder existing layers.

intermediate.question: How do cascades layers work with the @scope at-rule?
intermediate.answer: Layers and @scope interact; @scope styles are evaluated within their layer scope, and layers determine the base priority before scoping applies.

intermediate.question: How do layers affect the CSS cascade algorithm?
intermediate.answer: Layers add a new step in the cascade: after origin and !important, layers are evaluated before specificity and source order.

intermediate.question: How do you prioritize utility styles over component styles using layers?
intermediate.answer: Define utilities in a later layer: @layer base, components, utilities;.utilities override components of equal specificity.

intermediate.question: Can you use @layer in inline styles or style attributes?
intermediate.answer: No, @layer is an at-rule and cannot be used in inline styles or style attributes; it only works in stylesheets.

intermediate.question: How do you debug layer conflicts in DevTools?
intermediate.answer: Chrome DevTools shows layer information in the Styles panel; rules are labeled with their layer name to identify the source.

advanced.question: How does the cascade layer algorithm handle anonymous layers?
advanced.answer: Anonymous layers created without a name (@layer { ... }) have lower priority than named layers and cannot be referenced later.

advanced.question: How do you integrate CSS layers with a design token system?
advanced.answer: Load token-based styles in a low-priority layer (e.g., @layer tokens) and override with component-specific styles in higher layers.

advanced.question: How does @layer interact with the CSS Object Model (CSSOM)?
advanced.answer: CSSOM exposes layer information through CSSLayerStatementRule and CSSLayerBlockRule, allowing programmatic layer inspection.

advanced.question: How do cascade layers interact with shadow DOM encapsulation?
advanced.answer: Each shadow root has its own layer stack; layers inside shadow DOM do not affect or get affected by layers in the light DOM.

advanced.question: How do you create a layer reset strategy without duplicating selectors?
advanced.answer: Use @layer reset to wrap a full reset stylesheet, ensuring reset styles have the lowest priority without increasing specificity.

advanced.question: What happens when layers are defined in different CSS files loaded in different orders?
advanced.answer: Layer order is determined by the first time @layer declares the order across all loaded stylesheets, regardless of load timing.

advanced.question: How do you handle layer ordering when using CSS preprocessors like Sass?
advanced.answer: Preprocessors compile before browsers see the CSS; use @layer in your Sass output, managing layer order at the compiled CSS level.

advanced.question: How does @layer work with CSS animations and @keyframes?
advanced.answer: Keyframes are not affected by layer ordering since they are property value sources, not style declarations in the cascade.

advanced.question: How do cascade layers affect the CSS contain property?
advanced.answer: Layers do not directly affect containment; contain property scoping works independently of the cascade layer system.

advanced.question: How do you migrate a large codebase to use @layer without breaking existing styles?
advanced.answer: Place existing unlayered styles in a layer (they become layered), then add newer styles in higher-priority layers, testing incrementally.
